headline: Tech Rotation Swings Back Toward Software
summary: A trade that has punished software stocks in favor of semiconductors is reversing this week.  An iShares ETF tracking software stocks is up roughly 7% for the week, while another ETF tracking semiconductor producers has dropped about 5.7%—a more than 12-percentage-point gap on pace for the largest weekly software outperformance on record.  Software's beaten-down names such as ServiceNow, Intuit and Salesforce—which are each down more than 15% so far this year—were among the S&P 500's best-performing stocks Thursday.

headline: Does Corning (GLW) Using Gorilla Glass Ceramic 3 in Foldables Deepen Its Materials Moat?
summary: In early March 2026, Corning announced Gorilla Glass Ceramic 3, its toughest Gorilla Glass Ceramic to date, which will first appear on Motorola’s upcoming razr fold smartphone after extensive lab testing on concrete- and asphalt-like surfaces. This launch reinforces Corning’s role as a key materials supplier for advanced form factors like foldable devices, where durability is central to consumer appeal and manufacturer differentiation. Next, we’ll examine how Gorilla Glass Ceramic 3’s role...
